name: The Huntington National Bank
description_short: Huntington National Bank is a full-service bank offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, investing, and insurance products with FDIC protection and relationship-focused service.
description_long:
Huntington National Bank is a major U.S. bank operating under FDIC insurance backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank has expanded its reach through the recent acquisition of Cadence Bank, bringing operations across the Midwest and South with additional digital capabilities and regional presence.

Huntington offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the retail side, customers can access checking and savings accounts (including high-yield money market accounts at 3.20%-3.82% APY), credit cards, home/auto/personal loans, investing services, financial advisory, retirement planning, trusts, and insurance products (home, auto, and life). For business customers, they provide dedicated commercial banking and business checking accounts. The bank emphasizes online banking enrollment and digital account opening.

Huntington positions itself as a relationship-focused institution, marketing its "Perks Checking" account that grows with customers' needs and offers rate benefits tied to linked savings accounts. The bank also demonstrates community investment through partnerships like sponsoring PGA TOUR University for collegiate athletes and supporting the Ron Clark Academy and Pelotonia programs. Their website includes educational content on financial topics and weekly market analysis from wealth management experts.

As a large, established bank with FDIC insurance and broad product offerings, Huntington is a traditional banking option rather than a specialized lender. Customers seeking competitive rates or specialized services (like credit repair or emergency small loans) may find better options elsewhere, and the lack of specific APR/fee details on their website requires direct inquiry for accurate rate comparisons.
services: Money Market accounts (Huntington Relationship Money Market)
Online investing and financial advisory services
Home, auto, and life insurance products
Online banking and digital account management
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the U.S. Government full faith and credit
Competitive high-yield savings rates (3.20%-3.82% APY on Money Market, 2.98%-3.55% APY on Savings accounts)
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, credit cards, investing, and insurance in one institution
Online account opening and digital banking enrollment available
Relationship-based benefits through Perks Checking with tiered rate improvements for linked accounts
Expanded geographic reach and digital capabilities following Cadence Bank acquisition
Active community partnerships and educational financial content
cons: Specific minimum deposit requirements ($25,000 for Money Market, $5,000 for Savings) may exclude some customers
No APR, fee structures, or specific loan terms disclosed on website—requires direct inquiry
Large institution may lack personalized service compared to community banks or credit unions
Investment products explicitly noted as not FDIC-insured and may lose value
Website content does not detail customer service hours, branch locations, or account opening timeline
best_for: Customers seeking FDIC-protected deposits with competitive savings rates and full banking services
Business owners needing dedicated commercial banking and business checking accounts
Individuals wanting integrated banking, investing, and insurance under one institution
Customers in Huntington or Cadence Bank service areas (Midwest and South) preferring established bank relationships
diagnosis: Huntington is best for established customers or those new to banking seeking a traditional, full-service bank with FDIC protection, competitive savings rates, and integrated financial services. The main caveat is that detailed terms, APRs, and fees are not transparently displayed on their website, requiring direct contact for rate and cost comparisons.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
